item.title: Formula 1 drivers call Miami rule tweaks a step forward — but not nearly enough
item.description: What the tweaks will look like this weekend in Miami is still unclear

item.title: Aston Martin expects no Miami F1 'miracles' despite steps to fix vibration issues
item.description: Aston made use of the five-week break by taking one of its cars to Honda’s engine facility in Sakura.

item.title: How the Miami Grand Prix became a 'Super Bowl weekend': A 50ft 'yacht' and a hip hop museum
item.description: If the sport is to continue to grow in the U.S., the Miami Grand Prix believes it needs to create a festival-like experience for fans.

item.title: F1 knows its 2026 cars need fixing. Here's what's changing at the Miami GP
item.description: The Athletic has live coverage of the 2026 F1 Miami Grand Prix. Formula 1’s long-awaited return this weekend in Miami after a five-week break will bring a shift compared to the opening three races of the 2026 season. Not only have teams been working hard on their car upgrades to improve performance, but some rulebook tweaks are set to impact the on-track spectacle. Last week, the FIA announced a set of changes for Miami that chiefly focused on the electrical energy delivered by the all-new engines for 2026. These followed three meetings involving technical staff and team principals from...

item.title: McLaren Golf: Why is an F1 team making $485 golf clubs and is it a risk for Justin Rose?
item.description: The car manufacturer has switched to a new sport but can it really break into an already crowded marketplace?

item.title: Cadillac F1 hits the Miami GP with a major upgrade and something to prove to its home fans
item.description: The U.S. start-up arrives for its first race on home soil with a raft of improvements - but expects the rest of the grid to do the same

item.title: U.S. track star Abby Steiner suing Puma, Mercedes F1 over claim 'defective' shoes ended her career
item.description: Steiner, 26, alleges the products "are unsafe, unreasonably dangerous, defective and capable of causing injury."

item.title: Miami GP, a second season opener. Plus: Cadillac's new patriotic design
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: Formula 1 fan survey: Your thoughts on the new regulations, and the sport's future
item.description: We want your take on the 2026 cars, the quality of racing so far, how well you understand the new rules and where we go from here.

item.title: Running the London Marathon with Sebastian Vettel. Plus: Pierre Gasly and a $200K sponsor scam
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's Formula 1 newsletter.

item.title: F1 announces return of Turkish GP from 2027 on five-year deal
item.description: Turkey dropped off the full-time schedule in 2011 but will return next season to take the final free slot on the calendar.

item.title: The Iran War cost F1 two lucrative races, but the rest of the bill is still coming
item.description: As fuel and travel costs rise, what will continued Middle East uncertainty mean for F1?

item.title: Pierre Gasly's desperate trip to Uganda to fund an F1 dream: 'I'm not even sure he exists'
item.description: Most drivers have little chance of reaching F1. When Pierre Gasly urgently needed money to do this in 2016, he jumped at a mystery offer

item.title: McLaren CEO Zak Brown warns against increasing ownership links between F1 teams
item.description: The 54-year-old said the close technical relationships between teams could "compromise the integrity" of F1 and should be moved away from.

item.title: F1's latest rule tweaks, explained in plain terms. Plus: Mario Andretti wrote a column for us!
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's Formula 1 newsletter.

item.title: Doriane Pin just made F1 history for Mercedes. She wants more chances for women drivers
item.description: Doriane Pin - the 2025 F1 Academy champion - just tested a Mercedes F1 car. This made history

item.title: Liberty Media expanded F1's global audience. It plans to do it again with MotoGP
item.description: After conquering four-wheel motorsport, F1 commercial rights owner Liberty Media is now looking to two-wheelers and cracking the U.S. again

item.title: Rule changes aimed at fixing controversial new F1 cars agreed ahead of Miami GP
item.description: A series of rule changes to F1's controversial new cars has been agreed ahead of the Miami Grand Prix

item.title: FIA can’t let engine upgrades interfere with F1 championship fight - Toto Wolff
item.description: Teams could cut the deficit to Mercedes via the FIA’s engine catch-up system, ‘Additional Development and Upgrade Opportunities'.

item.title: How Godzilla-sponsored Haas became F1 2026's early giantkiller
item.description: The Haas F1 team has started the 2026 season as best-of-the-rest behind the big squads. But can it stay there over 19 more races?

item.title: Max Verstappen pays tribute to Juha Miettinen after racing driver dies in seven-car crash in Germany
item.description: Miettinen, a 66-year-old Finnish driver, died in the medical centre “after all attempts at resuscitation proved unsuccessful”.

item.title: Mario Andretti: Let me tell you why I don’t respect mediocrity
item.description: Mario Andretti, 86, is one of the best American drivers of all time. He is one of only two Americans to win the championship in Formula 1.

item.title: Some F1 ideas for the ‘Ocean’s 11’ prequel. Plus: Lewis Hamilton is just like us
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: Welcome to F1's new era of 'speed chess,' where every pass comes with a price
item.description: DRS is out. Overtake Mode is in. Here's why drivers say passing just got a lot harder

item.title: Why F1's boiling drama over engine compression ratios matters for the 2026 season
item.description: Has Mercedes found a loophole with its engines? And what exactly is the loophole, anyway?

item.title: F1's new cars behave unlike any others. It's all down to new aerodynamics
item.description: F1 has new cars in 2026, which look different to previous eras in grand prix racing. Here's how they work and what the drivers think

item.title: F1 hits reset in 2026. Your guide to the new cars, new rules and new era
item.description: Formula 1 evolves dramatically in 2026. Here's your year-long guide to it all

item.title: F1's new engines are the sport's biggest gamble in 12 years. Here's what changed
item.description: F1 is introducing new hybrid engines ahead of the 2026 season. Here's how they work and why they matter

item.title: How Formula 1's cost cap works: Rules, penalties and past breaches explained
item.description: The sport's $135 million spending limit aims to create parity — but compliance isn't always simple

item.title: F1 penalty points: How they work and why they're needed even though drivers have doubts
item.description: A deep dive into the details - and arguments - of Formula One penalty points for driver infractions

item.title: What are F1 driver development programs? Inside the academies that nurture future champions
item.description: All of F1's new 2025 drivers have one thing in common - they were part of a team’s driver development program or academy.

item.title: What are F1’s racing rules? A look at race stewards and drivers' battle for clarity
item.description: Multiple times during the 2024 season, drivers have faced questions — and raised their own — about what is and is not allowed on track.

item.title: How F1's Red Bull mastered the art of the 2-second pit stop
item.description: A deep dive into the positions, the practice, and the fine-tuned production that is the Formula One pit stop.

item.title: Welcome to Between the Racing Lines, your new guide to all things F1
item.description: F1 is complicated and confusing at times, and is constantly evolving. Here's your guide to keeping up.

item.title: Sim time, data crunching and Candy Crush: The life of an F1 reserve driver
item.description: Reserve drivers are more than simple substitutes — they play important roles within their teams, even if they rarely get to hit the track.

item.title: How F1 drivers train physically and mentally to go from 'gym fit' to 'race fit'
item.description: To have a chance at the title, drivers need not just a top-caliber car but to extract the maximum performance from it — and themselves.
